behaviors.description: Products marked out-of-stock must never appear in final recommendations, even if their rank score is high.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is filter+rank composition. A unit test on only the filter or only the ranker can miss ordering/merge bugs where out-of-stock items are reintroduced.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local catalog store with mixed stock statuses and high-scoring out-of-stock items, run the full recommendation pipeline, and assert no out-of-stock product IDs are present in returned pages.

behaviors.description: Blocked categories from user settings are excluded from recommendations.
behaviors.justification: Failure usually occurs at wiring boundary between user-settings retrieval and catalog filtering, which pure in-process mocks can hide.
behaviors.test_description: With a local user-settings source and catalog, configure blocked categories, execute recommendation generation, and verify all returned products are outside blocked categories.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns rank above products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This depends on feature extraction + ranking together; unit-testing scoring math alone can miss signal-join or feature pipeline failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create local fixtures where candidate set includes items with and without browsing-signal features, run end-to-end ranking, and assert signal-matching items are ordered ahead under controlled tie conditions.

behaviors.description: Pagination is correct and stable: repeated page-2 requests return identical results when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Stability bugs often come from sort/pagination interaction with storage ordering; mocks can hide nondeterministic ordering defects.
behaviors.test_description: Use a local DB-backed candidate query with deterministic seed data (including equal scores), request page 2 twice without writes, and assert identical ordered IDs and correct window boundaries.

behaviors.description: Serendipity rule: at least one recommended item comes from an unbrowsed category.
behaviors.justification: This is a policy constraint in the recommender pipeline, not LLM quality. Must verify final ranked output after diversification logic is applied.
behaviors.test_description: For a user with narrow browsing history, run recommendation generation against a diverse local catalog and assert at least one top-N result belongs to a category absent from user browsing history.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users receive popularity-based recommendations instead of empty results.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is fallback routing from empty personalization signals to popularity model plus data access; unit mocks can miss this branch integration.
behaviors.test_description: With a new-user profile and local popularity stats, execute recommendations and assert non-empty results are returned and align with popularity ordering constraints.

behaviors.description: Click tracking captures correct product ID, rank position, and timestamp per interaction.
behaviors.justification: Real failures are commonly schema/serialization/time-source/storage wiring issues that unit tests with mocked sinks can miss.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger recommendation display then simulated click events, persist to a local tracking store, and assert stored records contain exact product_id, shown_position, and valid event timestamp mapping to the clicked item.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: Performance SLA depends on real runtime/OS/process behavior and data-path costs; mocked or purely in-process micro-tests can hide true latency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommendation service in a production-like process with a 100k-product dataset, issue representative requests, and assert p95 (or defined metric) remains below 200ms under controlled load.

behaviors.description: After purchase, the purchased product is deprioritized in later recommendations.
behaviors.justification: This spans event ingestion/state update and subsequent ranking. Unit tests of either side alone can miss propagation failures.
behaviors.test_description: Generate baseline recommendations, record a purchase event for a recommended product in local state, regenerate recommendations, and assert the purchased item’s rank is lowered or removed per policy.

behaviors.description: Recommendations are deterministic for identical user state and no intervening activity.
behaviors.justification: Determinism issues typically arise from pipeline-level nondeterminism (unstable sort, random tie-breakers, clock leakage), not a single function.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed user/profile/catalog state and deterministic seeds/config, execute recommendation generation twice and assert identical ordered product ID lists and scores.
